decisions.summary: OKX REST liquidation backfill is disabled after research; official OKX changelog says the REST endpoint was delisted and WebSocket liquidation-orders channel should be used for real-time data.
Bybit all-liquidation WebSocket remains the MVP primary source for strategy liquidation signals when health checks pass.
Binance liquidation streams remain diagnostic because official docs describe snapshot-only semantics per 1000 ms window.
OKX WebSocket liquidation-orders can be collected as raw diagnostic data, but canonical liquidation_events remain disabled until instrument metadata is available for correct notional_usd.
OKX canonical normalization is allowed only when instrument metadata proves ctVal and ctValCcy support honest notional_usd calculation; otherwise OKX remains raw-only diagnostic.

OKX instruments metadata fetch is automated through scripts/fetch-okx-instruments.ps1 with ctVal/ctValCcy validation and UTF-8 without BOM output. A bounded OKX WebSocket run confirmed connectivity and dashboard diagnostic visibility; raw/canonical inserts still require an actual liquidation event in the probe window.

Bybit primary vs OKX diagnostic overlap validation is implemented as a coverage report, not event deduplication. OKX payloads for instruments missing validated metadata are stored raw-only instead of failing the collector.

A scheduled/manual GitHub Actions workflow runs bounded market-data diagnostics, fetches OKX metadata, captures collector status, writes an overlap report, writes a local trend report, and uploads artifacts without blocking normal push CI.

scripts/market-data-report-history.ps1 summarizes multiple nightly artifacts and classifies OKX usefulness as diagnostic-only evidence: insufficient-history, useful-diagnostic, healthy-but-sparse, unreliable-metadata, or unreliable-source.

scripts/check-api-docs-changelog.ps1 watches official Binance, Bybit, and OKX docs/changelogs against docs/research/api-docs-baseline.json and emits review-trigger artifacts in nightly market-data diagnostics.
Polymarket historical data limitations strengthen the requirement for a project-owned recorder and replay archive.
Hyperliquid hedge simulation must include dated fee schedule, funding model, slippage, and limited-depth assumptions.
ApeRAG Dev Memory uses project-owned FreeDeepseek completion, local embeddings, freshness checks, eval checks, and drift checks. OmniRoute is not part of the current ApeRAG route.
ApeRAG status drift is handled at source by project-owned image liquidation-aperag:local, which applies a build-time reconciler patch. Post-ingest status repair is not accepted.
